perturbation.original_text: THIS SECOND AMENDMENT TO CO-BRANDING AGREEMENT (this "Amendment") is made and entered into, effective for all purposes and in all respects as of the 23rd day of February, 1998, by and between PC QUOTE, INC., with its principal place of business at 300 South Wacker Drive, Chicago, Illinois 60605 ("PCQ") and A.B. Watley, Inc., with its principal place of business at 33 West 17th Street, New York, New York 10011 ("ABW").
perturbation.changed_text: THIS SECOND AMENDMENT TO CO-BRANDING AGREEMENT (this "Agreement Modification") is made and entered into, effective for all purposes and in all respects as of the 23rd day of February, 1998, by and between PC QUOTE, INC., with its principal place of business at 300 South Wacker Drive, Chicago, Illinois 60605 ("PCQ") and A.B. Watley, Inc., with its principal place of business at 33 West 17th Street, New York, New York 10011 ("ABW").
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Amendment' to 'Agreement Modification' creates confusion because the document later refers to 'the Agreement' ratified and confirmed. It's now unclear if 'the Agreement' refers to the original Co-Branding Agreement or this Agreement Modification, since the document refers to the original 'Agreement' multiple times. This creates an in-text contradiction on whether 'Amendment' and 'Agreement' are reffering to the same contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as modified hereby, the Agreement and its terms and provisions are hereby ratified and confirmed for all purposes and in all respects.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines the 'Amendment' as 'Agreement Modification'. The contradicted text refers to 'the Agreement' as being ratified and confirmed. Since the changed text redefines 'Amendment' to 'Agreement Modification' there is now uncertainty as to whether 'the Agreement' refers to the original agreement or the 'Agreement Modification', thus creating a contradiction within the document. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: To the extent, if any, that provision of this Agreement conflicts with or differs from any provision of the Agreement, such provision of this Second Amendment shall prevail and govern for all purposes and in all respects.
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent, if any, that provision of this Agreement conflicts with or differs from any provision of the Co-Branding Contract, such provision of this Second Amendment shall prevail and govern for all purposes and in all respects.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the second instance of 'Agreement' to 'Co-Branding Contract' introduces uncertainty. It is unclear if the 'Agreement' is the original Co-Branding Agreement, or this Second Amendment. This change creates an in-text contradiction on which contract provisions are being considered. This is made even more confusing since this text references 'this Second Amendment' as well.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as modified hereby, the Agreement and its terms and provisions are hereby ratified and confirmed for all purposes and in all respects.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity by referring to both 'Agreement' and 'Co-Branding Contract' within the same clause, making it unclear which agreement's provisions should prevail. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which ratifies and confirms 'the Agreement' and its terms. The change creates uncertainty about which 'Agreement' is being ratified (the original or as amended), thus creating an in-text contradiction as outlined in the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition.

perturbation.original_text: The text of Section 2. A. and B. of the Agreement is hereby deleted in its entirety and the following language is inserted in its stead: [***]
perturbation.changed_text: The text of Section 2. A. and B. of the Principal Accord is hereby deleted in its entirety and the following language is inserted in its stead: [***]
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Principal Accord' leads to confusion because 'Agreement' is used elsewhere to refer to the Co-Branding Agreement. This introduces uncertainty about which agreement's sections are being deleted and replaced, creating an in-text contradiction. If the 'Principal Accord' is something different, the ratification of the 'Agreement' is contradicotry since an element of said agreement is missing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as modified hereby, the Agreement and its terms and provisions are hereby ratified and confirmed for all purposes and in all respects.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'Principal Accord' introduces a significant ambiguity. The original text refers to modifying Section 2 of the 'Agreement'. The changed text alters this to 'Principal Accord'. The 'Contradicted Text' then ratifies the 'Agreement'. If the 'Principal Accord' is a separate document or concept from the 'Agreement', then ratifying the 'Agreement' while simultaneously modifying the 'Principal Accord' creates a contradiction. It introduces uncertainty as to which agreement is actually being ratified and which sections have been altered, thus satisfying the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'. This change breaks compatibility because the ratification of the 'Agreement' is made uncertain by the modification of what is either a part of it or something else entirely, which directly clashes with the clause that intends to ratify the agreement for all purposes.
